mission: L10N-001: Locale fallback chain for the i18n system

app.locale_type: Locale (18 locales: en, zh, zht, ko, de, es, fr, da, ja, pl, ru, uk, ar, no, br, th, bs, tr)
app.key_resolution: dynamic merge via createResource: merge(appDict, uiDict) returns { ...base, ...flatten(appDict), ...flatten(uiDict) }

desktop_renderer.t_function: export function t(key, params) { return translate(key, params) }; translate = i18n.translator(() => state.dict, i18n.resolveTemplate)
desktop_renderer.locale_type: Locale (16 locales: en, zh, zht, ko, de, es, fr, da, ja, pl, ru, uk, ar, no, br, bs)

console_app.locale_type: Locale (19 locales: en, zh, zht, ko, de, es, fr, it, da, ja, pl, ru, uk, ar, no, br, th, tr, bs)
console_app.key_resolution: i18n(locale) function returns { ...base, ...localeDict } via static imports but only defined for 18 non-en locales

web.t_function: N/A — uses JSON files loaded via content/

app_i18n.notes: Simplified Chinese; imports en for Keys type
Has 13 github.* keys but all marked // TODO: translate
Has 11 github.* keys; imports en for Keys type

console_i18n.notes: Italian — unique to console, not in app/desktop/ui

test_files.purpose: Tests resolveKey() with parent dict, base fallback, and template params. Defines the target fallback chain behavior.
Tests every locale resolves every key through parent→base→raw fallback chain. Coverage report in test output.
Tests every locale has all keys present from en.ts.

scripts.purpose: Generates stub entries for missing keys per locale.

app_context.description: Uses @solid-primitives/i18n translator. dict is a createResource that loads via merge(). Merged dict always has { ...base, ...flatten(appDict), ...flatten(uiDict) }. English keys always present.
app_context.missing_key_behavior: Returns undefined if key not in merged dict (never happens due to base merge)

desktop_renderer.description: build() at line 145-162 merges { ...base, ...flatten(appLocale), ...flatten(desktopLocale) } at init. All English keys always present for any locale.
desktop_renderer.missing_key_behavior: Returns undefined if key not in merged dict (never happens due to base merge)

console_app.description: i18n(locale) function returns spread merge of base + locale. All English keys always present.
console_app.missing_key_behavior: resolve(dict()[key], params) returns undefined if key missing (never happens due to base merge)

app.detectLocale: Checks navigator.languages against localeMatchers array (line 155). Comprehensive: includes th, tr, handles zh-Hant, nb/nn/no, pt, etc.
app.normalizeLocale: Returns value if in LOCALES array, else 'en' (line 181). No parent extraction from region codes.

desktop.detectLocale: Manual switch-style detection. Missing: th, tr locale entries. Handles zh-Hant → zht, nb/nn → no, pt → br.
desktop.normalizeLocale: N/A — uses parseLocale() with LOCALES array.

web.matchLocale: Most sophisticated. Handles localeAlias mapping (zh→zh-cn, zht→zh-tw, br→pt-br, nn→nb). Uses starts array for generic prefix matching.

parent_locale_extraction.current_state: No runtime parent locale extraction exists in app or desktop i18n systems. Only the test files define parent relationships (coverage.test.ts line 66: zht → zh).

no.reason: Norwegian Bokmål, no parent variant in our locale set

br.reason: Portuguese (Brazil), no pt variant in app locale set

region_code_extraction.pattern: For locale codes like zh-CN, zh-TW, pt-BR, nb-NO: extract primary language subtag (zh, pt, nb/nn, etc.) and map to our 2-letter codes.
region_code_extraction.existing_in_web: packages/web/src/i18n/locales.ts lines 28-62 has localeAlias mapping zh-cn → zh-cn, zh-tw → zh-tw, zht → zh-tw, br → pt-br

t_function_current_behavior.signature: t(key: keyof Dictionary, params?: Record<string, string | number>) => string
t_function_current_behavior.missing_key_at_translator_level: Returns undefined (not the key name) when key not in dict. @solid-primitives/i18n translator doesn't provide built-in fallback.
t_function_current_behavior.missing_key_resolved: Currently never happens because build()/merge() always includes all English keys via { ...base, ...localeDict } spread.
t_function_current_behavior.resolveTemplate: Handles {{ param }} substitution. Called for every key lookup regardless of dict source.

resolveKey_function.signature: resolveKey(key, dict, parentDict, base, params?) => string
chain: 1. if dict && key in dict: return resolveTemplate(dict[key], params)
2. if parentDict && key in parentDict: return resolveTemplate(parentDict[key], params)
3. if key in base: return resolveTemplate(base[key], params)
4. return key  (raw key as last resort)

coverage_test_assertions.description: For every locale, for every en key, resolves through chain and asserts result !== raw key and is non-empty string.

evidence.ja: Has 13 github.* keys but ALL marked // TODO: translate (identical to English)
evidence.zh: NO github.* keys (falls through to English via base merge)

github_key_coverage_gap.current_behavior: Due to { ...base, ...localeDict } merge, all keys are always present. Missing github.* keys fall through to English at the dict level, not the lookup level.
github_key_coverage_gap.desired_behavior: requested locale → parent locale (if available) → base (English) → raw key name

recommendation.approach: Implement resolveKey() as defined in fallback.test.ts and wire it into all three i18n subsystems

steps.action: Add parent locale map to each i18n runtime module
steps.detail: Define a PARENTS record mapping locale → parent locale (e.g., zht → zh). Add as lookup table next to existing LOCALES or loaders arrays.

steps.action: Replace the flat build()/merge() functions with resolveKey-style lookup
steps.detail: Instead of { ...base, ...localeDict }, keep dicts separate and chain lookups: t(key) → check locale dict → check parent dict → check base → return key. Use @solid-primitives/i18n's resolveTemplate for interpolation.

steps.action: Update locale detection to extract parent from region codes
steps.detail: Add normalizeLocale() function that handles zh-CN → zh, zh-TW → zht, pt-BR → br, nb-NO → no. Leverage patterns from packages/web/src/i18n/locales.ts localeAlias and matchLocale().

steps.action: Add th and tr to desktop locale list
steps.detail: Desktop LOCALES array and build() function are missing th and tr. Add them for parity with app locale set.

steps.action: Update tests to validate the new runtime behavior
steps.detail: Tests already define the contract. Minor updates may be needed after runtime changes.

effort_breakdown.core_logic_change: 2-3 hours: implement resolveKey and parent map in app/language.tsx
effort_breakdown.desktop_sync: 1-2 hours: replicate in desktop/renderer/i18n/index.ts + add th/tr
effort_breakdown.locale_detection: 1-2 hours: add normalizeLocale with region code extraction
effort_breakdown.testing: 1 hour: update tests and verify coverage per locale

existing_assets_leverage.test_files: packages/app/src/i18n/fallback.test.ts defines the exact resolveKey() function to implement
existing_assets_leverage.web_locale_alias: packages/web/src/i18n/locales.ts has localeAlias mapping and matchLocale() for region code extraction

risks_and_edge_cases.risk: Performance regression from three-dict fallback (current → parent → base) vs single merged dict lookup
risks_and_edge_cases.mitigation: Cache resolved keys per locale+parent combination. Profile with existing test suite.

risks_and_edge_cases.risk: Console/app and desktop use different i18n patterns (translator vs custom resolve)
risks_and_edge_cases.mitigation: Both can use the same resolveKey logic. For console, it's a simple key lookup without solid-primitives/i18n dependency.

risks_and_edge_cases.risk: Missing key returns key name string instead of undefined — could break components that check falsy
risks_and_edge_cases.mitigation: Returning the key name is safer than undefined (reduces null renders). Test for this specifically.

risks_and_edge_cases.risk: The { ...base, ...localeDict } merge currently makes ALL keys present. Changing to resolveKey approach could break components that rely on keys existing in the dict object (e.g., Object.keys iteration)
risks_and_edge_cases.mitigation: Audit for any code that iterates dict keys. Coverage test already checks that every key resolves successfully.

risks_and_edge_cases.risk: Desktop uses static imports (all 16 locales loaded at bundle time). Dynamic resolveKey adds no extra cost here.
risks_and_edge_cases.mitigation: No change needed for desktop import pattern. resolveKey is just a lookup change.

conclusion: The i18n system has three runtime implementations, all using the same { base + localeDict } merge pattern that ensures all English keys are always present. The roadmap asks for a proper parent-locale fallback chain (zht → zh → en) and region code normalization (zh-CN → zh). The exact desired behavior is already defined and tested in packages/app/src/i18n/fallback.test.ts and coverage.test.ts. Implementation just needs to wire resolveKey() into the three runtime subsystems.
